description: Unofficial taxis approach tourists at JKIA airport and malls quoting very high fares or taking long routes to inflate the bill
how_to_avoid: Use Uber Bolt or Little Cab apps always. Pre-book airport transfers through your hotel
what_to_do_if_it_happens: If you realise mid journey stay calm and insist on being dropped at your destination. Note the plate number
combined_text: Scam warning in Nairobi, Kenya: Fake Taxi. Description: Unofficial taxis approach tourists at JKIA airport and malls quoting very high fares or taking long routes to inflate the bill. How to avoid: Use Uber Bolt or Little Cab apps always. Pre-book airport transfers through your hotel. What to do: If you realise mid journey stay calm and insist on being dropped at your destination. Note the plate number.

description: A friendly local approaches you claims to recognise you and eventually asks for money food or to visit a shop where they earn commission
how_to_avoid: Politely decline conversations that feel overly familiar or that move toward requests for money or shopping
what_to_do_if_it_happens: Walk away calmly. Do not feel guilty. These are practised approaches
combined_text: Scam warning in Nairobi, Kenya: Friendship Scam. Description: A friendly local approaches you claims to recognise you and eventually asks for money food or to visit a shop where they earn commission. How to avoid: Politely decline conversations that feel overly familiar or that move toward requests for money or shopping. What to do: Walk away calmly. Do not feel guilty. These are practised approaches.

description: Someone approaches claiming to be a plainclothes police officer asking to inspect your money or passport for counterfeit or immigration checks
how_to_avoid: Real police rarely approach tourists this way. Ask to go to the nearest police station together to verify
what_to_do_if_it_happens: Do not hand over your passport or wallet. Ask for their badge number and insist on going to a station
combined_text: Scam warning in Nairobi, Kenya: Fake Police Officer. Description: Someone approaches claiming to be a plainclothes police officer asking to inspect your money or passport for counterfeit or immigration checks. How to avoid: Real police rarely approach tourists this way. Ask to go to the nearest police station together to verify. What to do: Do not hand over your passport or wallet. Ask for their badge number and insist on going to a station.

description: Crowded areas like Tom Mboya Street River Road and bus stations are hotspots for pickpockets working in teams
how_to_avoid: Keep phone and wallet in front pockets or a crossbody bag. Avoid showing expensive phones in busy streets
what_to_do_if_it_happens: If pickpocketed report to police for an insurance report. Cancel cards immediately
combined_text: Scam warning in Nairobi, Kenya: CBD Pickpocketing. Description: Crowded areas like Tom Mboya Street River Road and bus stations are hotspots for pickpockets working in teams. How to avoid: Keep phone and wallet in front pockets or a crossbody bag. Avoid showing expensive phones in busy streets. What to do: If pickpocketed report to police for an insurance report. Cancel cards immediately.

description: Persistent touts on Mombasa and Diani beaches offering tours boat rides craft and services often following tourists and becoming aggressive if refused
how_to_avoid: Firmly but politely say no once and keep walking. Do not engage in long conversations
what_to_do_if_it_happens: If someone becomes aggressive move toward your hotel or a busy area. Report to hotel security
combined_text: Scam warning in Mombasa, Kenya: Beach Boys. Description: Persistent touts on Mombasa and Diani beaches offering tours boat rides craft and services often following tourists and becoming aggressive if refused. How to avoid: Firmly but politely say no once and keep walking. Do not engage in long conversations. What to do: If someone becomes aggressive move toward your hotel or a busy area. Report to hotel security.

description: Craft sellers quote extremely inflated prices expecting tourists not to bargain leading to overpaying if you accept the first price
how_to_avoid: Always counteroffer at 30 percent of the asking price and work up from there. Walk away if price is not right
what_to_do_if_it_happens: Part of the local culture. Bargaining is expected and respected. Never feel obligated to buy
combined_text: Scam warning in Diani, Kenya: Overpriced Craft Market. Description: Craft sellers quote extremely inflated prices expecting tourists not to bargain leading to overpaying if you accept the first price. How to avoid: Always counteroffer at 30 percent of the asking price and work up from there. Walk away if price is not right. What to do: Part of the local culture. Bargaining is expected and respected. Never feel obligated to buy.

description: Quad bike hire companies damage deposits claiming pre-existing damage was caused by the customer to withhold large deposits
how_to_avoid: Photograph and video the quad bike thoroughly before and after. Use only recommended operators from your hotel
what_to_do_if_it_happens: Show your photos as evidence. Dispute with your credit card company if deposit wrongly withheld
combined_text: Scam warning in Diani, Kenya: Quad Bike Scam. Description: Quad bike hire companies damage deposits claiming pre-existing damage was caused by the customer to withhold large deposits. How to avoid: Photograph and video the quad bike thoroughly before and after. Use only recommended operators from your hotel. What to do: Show your photos as evidence. Dispute with your credit card company if deposit wrongly withheld.

description: Unofficial Maasai village visits where entrance fees are charged and then aggressive upselling of crafts occurs inside
how_to_avoid: Book cultural visits only through your lodge which uses vetted genuine community partnerships
what_to_do_if_it_happens: If uncomfortable leave immediately. Your lodge can intervene on your behalf
combined_text: Scam warning in Maasai Mara, Kenya: Maasai Village Scam. Description: Unofficial Maasai village visits where entrance fees are charged and then aggressive upselling of crafts occurs inside. How to avoid: Book cultural visits only through your lodge which uses vetted genuine community partnerships. What to do: If uncomfortable leave immediately. Your lodge can intervene on your behalf.

description: Someone approaches with a clipboard and an emotional story about orphans hospitals or schools asking for cash donations
how_to_avoid: Legitimate charities do not solicit on the street. If you want to donate research reputable organisations
what_to_do_if_it_happens: Decline politely and keep walking. Do not feel guilty
combined_text: Scam warning in Nairobi, Kenya: Charity Scam. Description: Someone approaches with a clipboard and an emotional story about orphans hospitals or schools asking for cash donations. How to avoid: Legitimate charities do not solicit on the street. If you want to donate research reputable organisations. What to do: Decline politely and keep walking. Do not feel guilty.

description: Unofficial spice tour guides approach tourists offering tours at low prices then demand much higher payment at the end
how_to_avoid: Book spice tours only through your hotel or a licensed tour operator. Agree on total price upfront in writing
what_to_do_if_it_happens: Refuse to pay more than agreed. Have the original agreed price saved as a message or note
combined_text: Scam warning in Zanzibar, Tanzania: Spice Tour Overcharging. Description: Unofficial spice tour guides approach tourists offering tours at low prices then demand much higher payment at the end. How to avoid: Book spice tours only through your hotel or a licensed tour operator. Agree on total price upfront in writing. What to do: Refuse to pay more than agreed. Have the original agreed price saved as a message or note.

description: Sellers approach with gemstones or jewellery claiming they are rare and valuable offering to let you resell them for profit in your home country
how_to_avoid: Gems sold on the street are almost never genuine. Do not get drawn into investment stories
what_to_do_if_it_happens: Walk away. The gems have no resale value
combined_text: Scam warning in Zanzibar, Tanzania: Gem and Jewellery Scam. Description: Sellers approach with gemstones or jewellery claiming they are rare and valuable offering to let you resell them for profit in your home country. How to avoid: Gems sold on the street are almost never genuine. Do not get drawn into investment stories. What to do: Walk away. The gems have no resale value.

description: Restaurants near tourist sites charge dramatically higher prices than local spots. Menus without prices are a warning sign
how_to_avoid: Ask for the menu with prices before sitting down. Walk a few streets away from major tourist spots for better prices
what_to_do_if_it_happens: If overcharged stay calm negotiate and pay a fair amount. Avoid confrontation
combined_text: Scam warning in Stone Town, Tanzania: Overpriced Restaurant. Description: Restaurants near tourist sites charge dramatically higher prices than local spots. Menus without prices are a warning sign. How to avoid: Ask for the menu with prices before sitting down. Walk a few streets away from major tourist spots for better prices. What to do: If overcharged stay calm negotiate and pay a fair amount. Avoid confrontation.

description: Unlicensed safari operators in Arusha approach tourists offering very cheap safaris that never materialise or are of very poor quality
how_to_avoid: Book only through TATO registered operators or through your hotel. Verify company registration. Avoid street touts
what_to_do_if_it_happens: If scammed report to Tanzania Tourist Board and your embassy. Stop payments immediately
combined_text: Scam warning in Arusha, Tanzania: Safari Booking Scam. Description: Unlicensed safari operators in Arusha approach tourists offering very cheap safaris that never materialise or are of very poor quality. How to avoid: Book only through TATO registered operators or through your hotel. Verify company registration. Avoid street touts. What to do: If scammed report to Tanzania Tourist Board and your embassy. Stop payments immediately.

description: Touts claim they can get discounted Kilimanjaro permits and disappear with your money
how_to_avoid: All Kilimanjaro permits must be booked through licensed operators registered with KINAPA
what_to_do_if_it_happens: Only book through registered operators. Pay by card where possible for chargeback protection
combined_text: Scam warning in Arusha, Tanzania: Kilimanjaro Permit Scam. Description: Touts claim they can get discounted Kilimanjaro permits and disappear with your money. How to avoid: All Kilimanjaro permits must be booked through licensed operators registered with KINAPA. What to do: Only book through registered operators. Pay by card where possible for chargeback protection.

description: Motorcycle taxi riders snatch phones bags or jewellery from passengers or pedestrians especially at junctions
how_to_avoid: Keep phone in pocket or bag when riding. Do not use phone on boda boda. Hold bag on your lap not hanging
what_to_do_if_it_happens: If robbed report to police. Note down any details of the rider or bike

Note down any details of the rider or bike.

description: Unofficial money changers on the street offer attractive rates then shortchange you using sleight of hand
how_to_avoid: Use only banks or licensed forex bureaus inside shopping malls. Never change money on the street
what_to_do_if_it_happens: Count your money carefully before leaving the counter. Discrepancies are hard to dispute once you walk away
combined_text: Scam warning in Kampala, Uganda: Money Exchange Scam. Description: Unofficial money changers on the street offer attractive rates then shortchange you using sleight of hand. How to avoid: Use only banks or licensed forex bureaus inside shopping malls. Never change money on the street. What to do: Count your money carefully before leaving the counter. Discrepancies are hard to dispute once you walk away.

description: Card skimming devices are sometimes placed on ATMs to steal card details especially in less monitored locations
how_to_avoid: Use ATMs inside bank branches or shopping malls only. Cover the keypad when entering PIN. Check for tampering
what_to_do_if_it_happens: Cancel your card immediately if you suspect skimming. Contact your bank and monitor transactions
combined_text: Scam warning in Nairobi, Kenya: ATM Skimming. Description: Card skimming devices are sometimes placed on ATMs to steal card details especially in less monitored locations. How to avoid: Use ATMs inside bank branches or shopping malls only. Cover the keypad when entering PIN. Check for tampering. What to do: Cancel your card immediately if you suspect skimming. Contact your bank and monitor transactions.

description: Tuk tuk drivers quote reasonable prices then add extra charges at the destination for luggage extra passengers or fuel
how_to_avoid: Agree on the total final price before boarding. Confirm it includes everything
what_to_do_if_it_happens: Firmly pay only what was agreed. Have exact change ready to avoid disputes over change
combined_text: Scam warning in Mombasa, Kenya: Tuk Tuk Overcharging. Description: Tuk tuk drivers quote reasonable prices then add extra charges at the destination for luggage extra passengers or fuel. How to avoid: Agree on the total final price before boarding. Confirm it includes everything. What to do: Firmly pay only what was agreed. Have exact change ready to avoid disputes over change.

description: Boat operators take tourists for dhow rides then demand far more money than agreed mid ocean using isolation as leverage
how_to_avoid: Agree on exact price duration and route before boarding. Get it in writing if possible. Use hotel recommended operators
what_to_do_if_it_happens: Stay calm. Pay agreed amount only. Report to Lamu tourism office on return

How to avoid: Agree on exact price duration and route before boarding. Get it in writing if possible. Use hotel recommended operators. What to do: Stay calm. Pay agreed amount only. Report to Lamu tourism office on return.

description: Fake hotel listings on third party sites take bookings and payments for hotels that do not exist or are not as described
how_to_avoid: Book directly with hotels or through reputable platforms like Booking.com. Verify hotel exists by calling directly
what_to_do_if_it_happens: Contact your bank to reverse payment. Report to the platform where you booked
combined_text: Scam warning in Kisumu, Kenya: Hotel Booking Scam. Description: Fake hotel listings on third party sites take bookings and payments for hotels that do not exist or are not as described. How to avoid: Book directly with hotels or through reputable platforms like Booking.com. Verify hotel exists by calling directly. What to do: Contact your bank to reverse payment. Report to the platform where you booked.
